title: Maine Democratic Senate candidates unite behind abolishing ICE after shooting
description: The leading candidates to replace Graham Platner as the Democratic nominee to challenge Sen. Susan Collins are presenting themselves as fighters in his mold and calling for ICE to be abolished.

title: Republican campaigns see immigration as a winning issue for midterms
description: Republicans are spending more money and running more ads on immigration than Democrats are ahead of the November midterm elections, according to an NPR analysis of advertisement data.

title: World leaders meet in D.C. to discuss left-wing political violence
description: Leaders from roughly 60 countries gathered in Washington, DC, to discuss what top Trump administration officials are calling an urgent threat: far left terrorism.

title: Trump claims the country's voting systems are vulnerable in primetime address
description: In a primetime address Thursday night, President Trump raised familiar claims about election integrity just months ahead of a midterm election, where Republicans are expected to take losses.

title: Rep. Jim Himes reacts to Trump's speech on election security
description: NPR's A Martinez discusses President Trump's speech on election security with Connecticut Rep. Jim Himes, the ranking Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee.

title: Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.

title: An analysis of President Trump's address
description: President Trump gave a primetime address Thursday night in which he spoke about the integrity of U.S. elections — a topic he has spread disinformation about for much of his political career.

title: White House teleprompter operator placed on leave after betting on Trump speeches
description: A White House teleprompter operator has been placed on unpaid leave after his online prediction market activity, which showed he was placing wagers related to President Donald Trump’s public remarks, was flagged to federal regulators.

title: These battleground Senate candidates raised the most money
description: Democratic candidates are raising more money than Republicans in key Senate races, and Georgia Sen. Jon Ossoff (D) has the most money at his disposal at $42 million.

title: White House says ICE traffic stops will continue after deadly shootings
description: The comments came following widespread reports earlier in the week that those stops would be put on pause after two immigrants were fatally shot by ICE agents in early July.

title: In primetime speech, Trump doesn't provide evidence for illegal voting
description: President Trump raised claims that the country's voting systems are vulnerable to being "rigged and stolen," without providing new evidence of a single fraudulent vote cast in any election.

title: President Trump to deliver prime time address on elections, though details are slim
description: President Trump is scheduled to deliver a primetime address from the White House focused on election security, though details about the speech are slim.

title: Officials probe whether White House teleprompter operator profited off Trump's words
description: It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.

title: House vote exposes Democrats’ widening divide over Israel
description: The symbolic vote showed how far Democratic politics on Israel have shifted, as progressive candidates turn anger over Gaza and U.S. military aid into a campaign issue.

title: Key House Democrats voice support for amendment to cancel Israel defense funding
description: The announcements reflect a growing disconnect between the Democratic establishment, with its long-standing support for the U.S. ally, and the party’s left wing.
